title: ranunculus acidotus
intro: Ranunculus acidotus, also known as Butcher's Broom, is a flowering plant in the Ranunculaceae family. It is a perennial herb native to the mountainous regions of China and India, commonly used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is rich in saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting digestive function. Traditionally, Ranunculus acidotus is prepared as a decoction or tea, and it is also used in herbal remedies, such as tinctures and infusions.
